name: Brown Rice Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 cup mushroom broth or vegetable stock (or water)
6 ounces grated cheese (Jack, Muenster, Cheddar, or Gouda)
Fresh herbs for garnish (parsley, cilantro, thyme, marjoram)
recipeInstructions:
This was one of the most popular dishes at Greens Restaurant in San Francisco.
1. Cook rice as directed on package.
2. Set tofu aside to drain on a slanted board or pan.
3. Prepare vegetables: chop onion, carrots, celery, pepper, and zucchini into 1/2-inch square pieces; quarter mushrooms if small, cutting larger ones into sixths or eighths; cut tofu into 1/2-inch cubes.
4. Heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Fry onions until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
5. Add garlic, nutritional yeast (if using), cumin seeds, and salt. Stir to combine and cook for 1 minute.
6. Add carrots, celery, green pepper, and 1/2 cup of liquid; cover skillet and braise vegetables until they begin to soften, about 5 minutes.
7. Add zucchini and mushrooms; continue cooking 7-10 minutes until vegetables are nearly but not completely cooked. If the pan becomes dry, add more liquid as needed.
8. Combine vegetables with rice and cheese in a large bowl. Season with salt and plenty of freshly ground black pepper.
9. Gently fold in tofu cubes.
10. Transfer mixture to a lightly oiled casserole dish.
11. Add additional liquid if necessary to moisten the dish.
12. Cover with foil and bake at 350°F for 1/2 hour.
13. Remove foil and bake for an additional 15 minutes.
14. Garnish with fresh herbs before serving.
